title: Add streamLoadedBuilds method to avoid full map copy in getEstimatedDurationCandidates
issue_text:
Fixes [#26596](https://github.com/jenkinsci/jenkins/issues/26596)

See issue for more context. `getLoadedBuilds()` is called by `getEstimatedDurationCandidates()` on every queue maintenance lock. `getLoadedBuilds()` copies the entire `core` map into a new tree map for every call, even though it only needs the 3 most recent completed builds, which is expensive for jobs with large build histories.

This PR adds `streamLoadedBuilds` to lazily stream over builds in memory instead of copying into a new map every queue maintenance cycle in `getEstimatedDurationCandidates`. Also simplifies `getLoadedBuilds` to call `streamLoadedBuilds`, which still does create a new map but is needed for plugin compatibility

### Testing done

It's a small change but I sanity checked it by running Jenkins locally before my change, added logging around the `loadedBuilds` and `getEstimatedDurationCandidates`, confirmed that new jobs lazily load 3 builds after examining 4 (since the 4th build is the current one in progress)

### Screenshots (UI changes only)

issue_report:
## slowness around `getEstimatedDurationCandidates` for large build histories

At my company, we have around 40,000 builds a day with an 8 day retention policy. It's also a monorepo with a single build phase called `ci-executor`. A single commit may trigger anywhere from a few builds to a thousand `ci-executor` builds depending on what changed, where each `ci-executor` dynamically determines which test groups to run. Therefore we get a lot of build records accumulate instead of being split up by repo and build/test/release phase.

We've noticed Jenkins master can be slow to schedule commits that require a large number of `ci-executor` builds, and I suspect it may be due to `getEstimatedDurationCandidates`. I looked at the queue logs and `getEstimatedDurationCandidates` takes a second or two, and it's called in queue.maintain() which locks the queue in between job submissions, adding overhead between each submission.

`getEstimatedDurationCandidates` calls `getLoadedBuilds()` to create a sorted map of builds using `this.core` (which is `nlogn` time; for us this is n=320k!). 

https://github.com/jenkinsci/jenkins/blob/960dc7d5ace67157d5c7a562601136890760f6e0/core/src/main/java/jenkins/model/lazy/LazyBuildMixIn.java#L275

This is just to select 3 most recent candidates that have finished. `this.core` is already sorted by newest first, so we can cut out building a sorted map each time with a helper function that iterates through `this.core` and selects 3 newest completed builds. 

https://github.com/jenkinsci/jenkins/blob/960dc7d5ace67157d5c7a562601136890760f6e0/core/src/main/java/jenkins/model/lazy/AbstractLazyLoadRunMap.java#L93

I think this should make Jenkins queue maintenance a lot faster for teams with a large build history.

Does this sound right? Let me know if I can provide any more additional info. I'm also happy to take a stab at it if this sounds like the right approach.
